title: allium fraseri
intro: Allium fraseri, also known as Fraser's garlic or Fraser's onion, is a perennial plant belonging to the onion and garlic family (Allium spp.). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The plant's active compounds, such as allicin and other sulfur-containing compounds, exhibit antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and boosting the immune system. Allium fraseri can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
